arts.ttl: Michael Carrick's bullish response to Middlesbrough January transfer decision question
arts.sum: Michael Carrick delivered a bullish defence of his decision not to sign another centre-back in the January transfer window, with Middlesbrough now facing a defensive injury crisis. But asked about the decision not to sign a centre-back in January beyond making Edmundson’s loan signing a permanent one, Carrick said: “You can only have so many players in your squad.

arts.ttl: Middlesbrough FC Set to End Kit Deal with Errea
arts.sum: Middlesbrough Football Club has officially announced that they will part ways with Italian sportswear manufacturer Errea at the conclusion of the 2024-25 season, ending their second partnership with the brand.

arts.ttl: Dael Fry, Rav van den Berg, Josh Dede and more - Boro injury update ahead of QPR
arts.sum: Fry was Boro ’s last remaining fit centre-back after injuries suffered by George Edmundson, Rav van den Berg and Luke Ayling. It’s a blow for Boro who now face two important games this week against QPR and Luton Town with no fit centre-backs.

arts.ttl: Middlesbrough appear to have made a decision on free-agent defender after trial last week
arts.sum: Boro now have two games this week before an international break, but they are likely to play both without a recognised senior centre-back against QPR and Luton, with Pieters not back at Rockliffe on Monday and therefore unlikely to be signed. Middlesbrough appear unlikely to pursue a deal to sign free-agent defender Erik Pieters after his trial at the club last week.

arts.ttl: “I’m not sure” – NT manager on player’s role at QPR, hint he’s not fulfilling expectations
arts.sum: On January 29th, Tottenham Hotspur’s Yang Min-hyeok joined Queens Park Rangers on loan until the end of this season. Last summer, Tottenham reached an agreement with Gangwon FC for the 18-year-old’s transfer, and he arrived in north London in January. arts.ttl: Middlesbrough's centre-back options including under-21s amid worsening injury crisis
arts.sum: Though he’s never done it for Boro until switching midway through the Derby game, Borges has played centre-back before in his career. Neto Borges played centre-back at Swansea, with Ryan Giles coming on at left-back.
